body:
{
  "title": "Hiring Panel — CTO Interview: Dani Osei",
  "compiled_truth": "Panel interview conducted on 2026-03-20 for the CTO position at [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor). Attendees were [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) and [Riley Moreau](people/riley-moreau). The candidate, Dani Osei, came in with a strong background in distributed systems and had previously led infrastructure teams at two mid-stage startups — one of which went through a messy acqui-hire, the other reaching Series C before pivoting away from its core product. Seren opened the panel with a deep-dive into Dani's experience managing eng teams through ambiguity, especially during periods where the technical roadmap was shifting under thier feet. Dani spoke at length about building consensus among senior ICs when there's genuine disagreement about architecture direction, and referenced a specific incident where a migration to event-driven patterns nearly derailed a product launch.\n\nRiley focused the second half on technical fit — specifically how Dani thinks about deterministic data infrastructure and the tradeoffs involved in single-pass primitives. Dani's answers were described in Riley's notes as \"surprisingly nuanced\" — they demonstrated familiarity with the design space without overselling their knowledge. One area of concern flagged by Riley was Dani's relative inexperience with the kind of ultra-low-level optimization work that Anchor's core stack demands. Dani acknowledged this openly and framed it as a growth area rather than a gap, which both panelists noted positively.\n\nSeren's overall assessment was a strong lean-yes, contingent on a follow-up conversation about team-building philosophy. Riley gave a qualified yes — noting that Dani's leadership instincts are excellent but wanting to see a technical take-home before committing. There was brief discussion about whether the CTO role at this stage should skew more toward people leadership or hands-on architecture. Both panelists agreed that Anchor needs someone who can do both for at least the first 18 months, and Dani seemed aware of that expectation.\n\nOne logistical note: the panel ran about 15 minutes over the scheduled 90 minutes because the architecture discussion got genuinely interesting. Riley flagged that Dani asked several pointed questions about Anchor's approach to authentication that suggested real depth of thinking. Post-panel debrief was short — roughly ten minutes — with both panelists agreeing to advance Dani to the next round pending the take-home excersize.",
  "timeline": [
    "- **2026-03-14** | Dani Osei's resume and portfolio forwarded to [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) and [Riley Moreau](people/riley-moreau) for review ahead of panel.",
    "- **2026-03-18** | Pre-panel alignment call between Seren and Riley to agree on question areas and evaluation criteria for CTO candidates.",
    "- **2026-03-20** | Hiring panel conducted. 105-minute session with Dani Osei covering leadership philosophy, technical depth, and cultural fit at [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor).",
    "- **2026-03-22** | Technical take-home prompt sent to Dani Osei, focusing on a simplified version of a single-pass pipeline design problem.",
    "- **2026-03-28** | Take-home submission received. [Riley Moreau](people/riley-moreau) reviews and scores it over the weekend.",
    "- **2026-04-02** | Final round interview scheduled — Dani meets the broader founding team for a half-day on-site.",
    "- **2026-04-10** | Offer extended to Dani Osei for CTO role. Two-day deliberation window.",
    "- **2026-04-12** | Dani Osei accepts the CTO offer. Start date set for 2026-05-01.",
    "- **2026-06-15** | Dani presents first architectural review to the team, incorporating feedback from the original panel discussion about authentication patterns."
  ]
}
